subject: Try the Goose RPI recipes this week — Commander will suggest them
body:
Quick reminder: the Goose RPI recipes (Research, Plan, Implement, Iterate) are installed and Commander will now suggest them automatically when your task matches.

You DON'T need to type any slash commands yourself. Just give Commander a real task this week. When it spots a match, it will say something like:

   'This looks like a Goose RPI plan task — want me to use it? reply "rpi" or "freehand".'

You just approve or skip. The more sessions where you let it suggest, the more data the watchdog has for the Day 7 review. Without real usage, that review will have nothing to chew on.
what_to_do: Open Claude Code in whatever project you're working on this week. Activate HQ. Then just give Commander a non-trivial task as you normally would. When it proposes a Goose RPI recipe, reply 'rpi' to try it or 'freehand' to skip.

subject: 1-week review of the Goose RPI pilot — time to decide
body:
It's been 7 days since the HQ Watchdog and Goose RPI recipes went live. The watchdog has been measuring every session — including any where you let Commander use the Goose RPI recipes vs sessions you took freehand.

Three possible outcomes today:

   1. The Goose RPI recipes helped → merge goose-recipes-v1 to main.
   2. They didn't help (or you didn't use them enough) → extend the trial another week.
   3. They actively hurt → revert (switch claude-hq back to watchdog-v1).

The right call is data-driven, not feel-driven. The watchdog knows what happened.
what_to_do:
Open Terminal and run:

   cd ~/claude-hq && claude

Then paste this exactly into Claude Code:

   Activate HQ. Look at the watchdog data from the past 7 days. Compare sessions where I used the Goose RPI recipes against sessions where I went freehand. Recommend: merge goose-recipes-v1 to main, extend the trial, or revert. Show me the numbers behind the recommendation.

subject: Time to give PATS-Copy its own architectural watchdog
body:
Phase 2 (extracting the HQ Watchdog into a reusable template) was scoped to wrap up around now. If that's done, the next step is the first real project pilot: spawn an architectural watchdog for PATS-Copy.

It starts in 'observe-only' mode for the first 14 days so it absorbs the current state of the codebase before warning. Without that soak window, you'd drown in alerts about historical code that already shipped.

This is the load test for the whole project-native watchdog architecture — the design docs you wrote on Day 0 (~/claude-hq/docs/project-watchdog/) will get their first real workout.
what_to_do:
Open Terminal and run:

   cd ~/Desktop/POLYMARKET_TRADING_3.0 && claude

Then paste this exactly into Claude Code:

   Activate HQ. Spawn the project-native architectural watchdog for PATS-Copy. Use the schema in ~/claude-hq/docs/project-watchdog/. Fingerprint PATS-Copy as (paper, realtime, many, multi-source, never, costly). Start in observe-only mode for 14 days so it absorbs the current state before warning.

subject: PATS observe-only window ending — ready to flip the watchdog to active?
body:
The PATS-Copy architectural watchdog has been silently observing for 14 days. It now has a picture of what the codebase looks like and what 'normal' development on it looks like.

Flipping to active means it starts surfacing alerts when it sees architectural drift — type drift, single-source-of-truth violations, state inconsistency. The kinds of issues that surfaced as bugs in the position-tracking work earlier this year.

Before flipping, it's worth reviewing the backlog of would-have-fired alerts so nothing surprising happens on day one.
what_to_do:
Open Terminal and run:

   cd ~/Desktop/POLYMARKET_TRADING_3.0 && claude

Then paste this exactly into Claude Code:

   Activate HQ. The PATS-Copy architectural watchdog has been observing for 14 days. Show me the backlog of would-have-fired alerts. Then flip the watchdog from observe-only to active alerting. If any alerts in the backlog look like real issues we should fix first, surface them.

subject: PATS watchdog stable — wire the spawn into Commander Step 0?
body:
If the PATS architectural watchdog has been running cleanly for a week without too many false alarms, the next step is to make project-watchdog spawning automatic for every NEW project — not just retrofits.

Wiring it into Commander Step 0 means: from this point onward, every brand-new project bootstrap will instantiate its own architectural watchdog at creation time. Same pattern PATS-Copy got, but at the moment of spawn rather than retrofit.

This is the moment the architecture stops being a pilot and becomes the default.
what_to_do:
Open Terminal and run:

   cd ~/claude-hq && claude

Then paste this exactly into Claude Code:

   Activate HQ. The PATS-Copy architectural watchdog has been stable for a week. Modify Commander Step 0 (project bootstrap) so every new project automatically gets its own project-native architectural watchdog at creation. Use the schema in ~/claude-hq/docs/project-watchdog/. Update the Decision Log when you're done.
